data.title: OpenEden's official website and portal DNS servers appear to have been compromised.
data.description: RWA tokenization platform OpenEden issued a statement on the X platform stating that its official website and portal's DNS service was suspected of being compromised. Users are advised to refrain from any interaction with the aforementioned domains to prevent damage to their wallet assets. All reserve assets are currently secure and under control. The team is investigating the matter and will provide updates as soon as possible.

data.title: Alibaba Unveils Enhanced Qwen for AI Agent Tasks
data.description: Alibaba has introduced the latest version of its AI model, Qwen, aimed at improving the capabilities of AI agents. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this development is part of Alibaba's ongoing efforts to advance artificial intelligence technology. The updated Qwen model is expected to enhance the performance of AI-driven tasks, providing more efficient solutions across various applications. This move aligns with Alibaba's strategy to leverage AI in boosting productivity and innovation within its operations and services. The company continues to focus on integrating AI into its business processes, reflecting the growing importance of artificial intelligence in the tech industry.

data.title: Significant SOL Transfer to Fireblocks Custody
data.description: A substantial transfer of SOL tokens has been reported. According to ChainCatcher, data from Arkham indicates that at 16:55, a total of 23,500 SOL were moved from an anonymous address, starting with 2DtqsL3R, to Fireblocks Custody. This transaction highlights ongoing activity within the cryptocurrency market.

data.title: Katch Seeks New Funding Amid KLIF Closure
data.description: Katch is actively pursuing new funding opportunities as it prepares to close KLIF. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the company's strategic move to secure financial backing during this transition. The decision to wind down KLIF is part of Katch's broader efforts to streamline operations and focus on core business areas. This development comes at a time when the company is evaluating its financial strategies to ensure long-term sustainability. Katch's leadership is optimistic about attracting investors who align with their vision for future growth. The closure of KLIF marks a significant shift in the company's operational focus, as it seeks to optimize resources and enhance its market position.

data.title: Ukrainian Ex-Energy Minister Implicated in Corruption Probe
data.description:
A former Ukrainian energy minister has been identified as a suspect in a significant corruption investigation. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the ongoing efforts by Ukrainian authorities to tackle corruption within the country's energy sector. The investigation is part of a broader initiative to address systemic corruption issues that have plagued Ukraine for years.

The former minister's involvement in the case underscores the challenges faced by the Ukrainian government in its attempts to reform the energy sector and improve transparency. Authorities are focusing on uncovering any illicit activities and holding those responsible accountable.

This development comes amid increased scrutiny of Ukraine's energy policies and practices, as the country seeks to align itself more closely with European standards. The investigation is expected to have significant implications for Ukraine's energy industry and its efforts to combat corruption.

data.title: Russia's Finam Registers Cryptocurrency Mining Investment Fund
data.description: Russia's retail brokerage firm Finam has announced the registration of a cryptocurrency mining investment fund with the country's central bank. According to Odaily, the fund is currently in the final stages of operational preparation. Finam plans to introduce a series of investment products related to digital asset mining in the future.

data.title: Digital Asset Funds Experience Fourth Week of Outflows Amid Market Shifts
data.description:
Digital asset investment funds experienced a net outflow of $173 million last week, marking the fourth consecutive week of withdrawals. According to NS3.AI, U.S. funds were particularly affected by significant outflows, while Europe and Canada saw inflows, primarily driven by Germany, Canada, and Switzerland.

Bitcoin and Ethereum products notably faced net outflows during this period. In contrast, altcoins such as XRP, SOL, and LINK attracted inflows, indicating a shift in investor interest. Additionally, trading volumes saw a sharp decline, reflecting the ongoing market adjustments.

data.title: French listed company Capital B increased its holdings by 6 BTC, bringing its total holdings to 2,834 BTC.
data.description: French listed company Capital B disclosed that it has increased its holdings by 6 BTC, bringing its total Bitcoin holdings to 2,834 BTC, ranking 28th among listed Bitcoin treasury companies. (BitcoinTreasuries)

data.title: Russian retail brokerage firm Finam registers cryptocurrency mining investment fund
data.description: Russian retail brokerage firm Finam has disclosed that it has registered an investment fund focused on cryptocurrency mining with the country's central bank. The fund is currently in the final stages of operational preparation and will launch a series of investment products related to digital asset mining in the future. (Bitsmedia)

data.title: Trezor and Ledger users are being targeted by physical mail scams that trick them into scanning QR codes to steal their mnemonic phrases.
data.description: Trezor and Ledger users have been targeted by physical mail scams. Scammers impersonate official agencies, tricking users into scanning QR codes to access phishing websites. They then use pretexts such as "identity verification" and "transaction checks" to obtain mnemonic phrases, ultimately stealing cryptocurrency assets. (Bleepingcomputer)

data.title: Scammers are sending physical letters to Ledger and Trezor users, posing as official support and directing victims to phishing sites to steal recovery seed phrases. The campaign uses realistic branding and urges users to never share or enter seed phrases online.
data.description:
null
source: https://x.com/wublockchain/status/2023317915122975042

data.title: New Wallet Withdraws 71.84 Million SIREN Tokens from CEX
data.description: A newly created wallet has withdrawn 71.84 million SIREN tokens from a centralized exchange (CEX). According to ChainCatcher, this transaction currently shows an unrealized profit of $8.98 million.

data.title: BOJ Governor Ueda and PM Takaichi Discuss Economic Matters
data.description: Bank of Japan Governor Kazuo Ueda held a routine meeting with Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi to address economic issues. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the significance of these discussions in shaping Japan's economic policies. The meeting focused on current economic conditions and potential strategies to enhance growth and stability. Both leaders emphasized the importance of collaboration between the government and the central bank to navigate economic challenges. The discussions are part of ongoing efforts to ensure Japan's economic resilience amid global uncertainties.

data.title: Scammers Target Ledger and Trezor Users with Phishing Letters
data.description: Scammers are sending physical letters that mimic official support from Ledger and Trezor, leading recipients to phishing websites aimed at stealing wallet recovery seed phrases. According to NS3.AI, these fraudulent letters are part of a broader scheme to compromise cryptocurrency security. Users are advised to avoid sharing or entering their seed phrases online to safeguard their assets.

data.title: Market Sentiment Remains Fearful as Crypto Index Shows Slight Increase
data.description: The crypto market's Fear and Greed Index has seen a slight increase to 12, yet it continues to reflect 'Extreme Fear' among investors. According to NS3.AI, this index is derived from various factors such as volatility, market volume, social media sentiment, market surveys, Bitcoin dominance, and Google Trends analysis. Despite the marginal rise, the prevailing sentiment remains one of caution, indicating that investors are still wary of the market conditions.

data.title: The crypto market's Fear & Greed Index rose to 12, remaining in the "extreme fear" range.
data.description: According to Alternative data, the Fear & Greed Index for the crypto market rose slightly to 12 today, remaining in the "extreme fear" range. Note: The Fear & Greed Index ranges from 0 to 100 and includes the following indicators: volatility (25%) + market volume (25%) + social media buzz (15%) + market surveys (15%) + Bitcoin's share of the overall market (10%) + Google trending topics (10%).

data.title: SBI Holdings Clarifies Investment in Ripple
data.description: SBI Holdings CEO Yoshitaka Kitao has addressed recent claims regarding the company's investment in Ripple's XRP token. According to NS3.AI, Kitao clarified that SBI Holdings has not invested $10 billion in XRP tokens. Instead, the company holds approximately 9% equity in Ripple Labs. The CEO highlighted that SBI Holdings does not directly own XRP tokens but supports Ripple through its significant equity stake and partnership. This statement underscores SBI's strategic emphasis on Ripple's blockchain technology rather than the XRP cryptocurrency itself.

data.title: Republic Technologies Targets $3 Million Through Private Placement
data.description: Republic Technologies, a company listed in Canada and known for its Ethereum treasury holdings, has announced a private placement initiative. According to NS3.AI, the company plans to raise $3 million by issuing over 14 million special warrants priced at $0.21 each. Republic Technologies currently holds approximately 1,570.6 ETH, which was acquired at an average price of $2,700 and is now valued at around $3.2 million. This strategic move is intended to bolster the company's financial standing as part of its ongoing Ethereum treasury strategy.

data.title: Iran's Foreign Minister to Meet UN Nuclear Watchdog Chief
data.description: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi is scheduled to meet with the head of the United Nations' nuclear watchdog. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the significance of this meeting amid ongoing discussions about Iran's nuclear program. The meeting is expected to address key issues related to nuclear inspections and compliance with international agreements. This development comes as global attention remains focused on Iran's nuclear activities and their implications for regional and international security. The outcome of the meeting could influence future diplomatic engagements and negotiations concerning Iran's nuclear ambitions.
